name: Tamales
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the pork in water until tender. Remove the meat and set aside, reserving some of the broth for the dough and chili.
2. Chop the meat into 1/4-inch pieces and place it in a pan.
3. Dissolve chile powder in 1.5 cups of the reserved broth, then add to the meat along with garlic, spices, and salt. Cook until done.
4. Cream the lard in a mixing bowl. Add masa harina and mix. Gradually incorporate enough broth to make the dough spreadable with a table knife.
5. Rinse corn husks and soak them in water until they are pliable.
6. Spread about 2 tablespoons of masa on the center of each husk. Place a portion of the chili meat mixture on top.
7. Fold the sides of the husk toward the center, then fold the bottom up and the top down.
8. Tie each tamale securely with narrow corn husks strips.
9. Fill a large kettle with 2 inches of water and place a rack in it. Steam the tamales for about 40 minutes.
